architecture.summary: LAVE is a constrained decoding system for diffusion LLMs (dLLMs) that enforces context-free grammar (CFG) compliance during non-autoregressive generation. The core mechanism is lookahead-then-verify: when the dLLM proposes a token for a masked position in an incomplete prefix, LAVE samples multiple complete prefixes by filling remaining masked positions using the model's predicted token distributions. These sampled prefixes are validated against the CFG using a grammar parser (Earley or LR). A token is accepted only if at least one sampled complete prefix can be extended into a valid sentence under the grammar. If proposal fails repeatedly (exceeding an attempt budget), a cache-enhanced recovery mechanism resets to a previously verified prefix and generates an additional token sampled from the set of grammar-admissible successors, preserving both non-autoregressive behavior and constraint reliability.

biblio.title: Lookahead-then-Verify: Reliable Constrained Decoding for Diffusion LLMs under Context-Free Grammars

claims.statement: Current approaches specifically designed for dLLMs may allow intermediate outputs that are impossible to complete into valid sentences, which significantly limits their reliability in practice.
Dream-v0-Instruct-7B exhibits a syntax error rate of up to 23.8% on HumanEval-CPP.
With a lookahead size of 10, the acceptance rate reaches 98.1% for Dream-v0-Instruct-7B and 97.3% for LLaDA-8B-Instruct in preliminary experiments validating lookahead-then-verify feasibility.
On SMILES-Bench with LLaDA-8B, LAVE raises syntactic@1 from 66.0% to 98.7%, syntactic@3 from 74.1% to 99.4%, syntactic@5 from 75.9% to 100.0%, and syntactic@10 from 78.9% to 100.0%.
On CPP-Bench with DiffuC-7B, LAVE increases syntactic@1 from 79.9% to 97.0%, syntactic@3 from 80.2% to 99.1%, syntactic@5 from 80.5% to 99.6%, and syntactic@10 from 81.7% to 100.0%.
On CPP-Bench with Dream-7B, LAVE outperforms the best baseline IG-CD by 8.4% in terms of syntactic@1 (96.0% vs 87.6%).

LAVE consistently outperforms existing baselines and achieves substantial improvements in syntactic correctness across four widely used dLLMs (LLaDA-8B, LLaDA-1.5, Dream-7B, DiffuC-7B) and three representative benchmarks (CPP-Bench, JSON-Bench, SMILES-Bench), while incurring negligible runtime overhead.

LAVE benefits more from increasing k than FS-CD. On CPP-Bench with LLaDA-1.5, as k increases from 1 to 10, LAVE improves syntactic@k from 91.3% to 98.8%, whereas FS-CD shows only a marginal increase from 73.8% to 74.2%.
